mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 5.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.1 million units signals a strengthening housing market that boosts consumer spending and corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 1.1M are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 105 signals a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profits and boosts inventory levels, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < 105 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 4.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decrease in initial jobless claims below 200,000 signals a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS < 200K are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income above 5% signals a strengthening consumer market that boosts corporate profits and equity prices, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with RSAFS > 5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the GDP below 160 billion signals a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profits and boosts inventory levels, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 < 160B are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.
